skill.name: plan-harder
skill.description: Create detailed, phased implementation plans with sprints and atomic tasks for bugs, features, or development tasks.
skill.summary: Generate comprehensive implementation plans with sprint breakdowns, atomic tasks, and validation checklists.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' patterns were markdown code examples using backticks for inline code formatting, not actual shell execution. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' detections were YAML syntax ('blocker') confused with BlockCipher. This is a pure documentation/planning skill with no executable code, scripts, or security risks.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Examples
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected markdown inline code examples (using backticks) as potential Ruby/shell command execution. Lines 71, 74-75 show filename transformation examples in markdown format, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct inspection confirms these are markdown code examples showing text transformation patterns, not executable shell commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: YAML Syntax Confused with Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected YAML syntax elements (the '>' character and 'blocker' text in line 88) as weak cryptographic algorithms. These are standard YAML multi-line string syntax and plain English text, not cryptographic references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Context confirms these are YAML syntax characters and English words, not cryptographic algorithm references.

content.value_statement: Break down complex features into manageable sprints with atomic tasks. Each task includes clear acceptance criteria, dependencies, and validation methods for predictable project delivery.
actual_capabilities: Research codebase architecture and patterns before planning
Ask targeted questions to clarify requirements and scope
Break down features into demoable sprints with atomic tasks
Generate structured plan documents with markdown templates
Identify potential issues and edge cases proactively
Coordinate subagent review of plans for quality assurance
limitations: Only creates plans, does not implement code or features
Requires clear user requirements to generate effective plans
Plan quality depends on codebase accessibility and complexity
Cannot execute tasks or validate plans automatically

use_cases.title: Feature Implementation Planning
use_cases.description: Break down new feature requests into sprint phases with atomic, committable tasks that include dependencies and validation criteria.

use_cases.title: Bug Fix Strategy
use_cases.description: Create structured approaches to resolving complex bugs by analyzing root causes and defining step-by-step resolution paths.

use_cases.title: Technical Project Planning
use_cases.description: Plan technical initiatives, migrations, or refactoring efforts with phased approaches and rollback strategies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feature Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan harder for implementing user authentication with email and password
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs a complete implementation plan for a standard feature

prompt_templates.title: Complex System Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan harder for building a real-time notification system with websockets, email backups, and user preferences
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to plan a multi-component feature with various technical considerations

prompt_templates.title: Bug Resolution Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan harder for fixing the race condition in the payment processing workflow that causes double charges
prompt_templates.scenario: User has identified a complex bug and needs a structured fix approach

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan harder for migrating from REST API to GraphQL while maintaining backward compatibility for existing clients
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to plan a significant architectural change with minimal disruption

output_examples.input: Plan harder for adding dark mode to the application
output: Plan includes 3 sprints: CSS variable setup, component updates, and user preferences
Each task specifies file locations, complexity rating, and acceptance criteria
Includes testing strategy and rollback plan if issues arise

output_examples.input: Plan harder for implementing file upload with drag-and-drop
output: Sprint 1: Backend file storage and validation
Sprint 2: Frontend upload component with progress tracking
Sprint 3: Drag-and-drop UI and file management
Plan includes edge cases for file size limits and security considerations

best_practices: Provide clear, specific requirements when invoking the skill to improve plan quality
Review the generated plan carefully and ask for clarification on any ambiguous items
Use the atomic task breakdown to track progress and manage development workflow
Keep the generated plan file as reference during implementation and update as needed
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for simple, one-line changes that do not require planning
Do not expect the skill to implement code or execute the plan automatically
Do not skip the requirement clarification phase when needs are ambiguous
Do not treat the plan as immutable - update it as you learn more during implementation

faq.question: When should I use 'plan harder' instead of regular planning?
faq.answer: Use this skill when you need detailed, phased implementation plans with atomic tasks. Regular planning works for simple tasks, but plan harder provides sprint breakdowns, dependencies, and validation checklists for complex features.

faq.question: Does this skill implement the code for me?
faq.answer: No. Plan harder only creates detailed implementation plans. You or another agent must implement the actual code based on the plan structure.

faq.question: What makes a task 'atomic'?
faq.answer: An atomic task is small, independent, and committable. It can be completed in one session, has clear inputs and outputs, and includes validation criteria. For example, 'Add OAuth config to env variables' is atomic, while 'Implement authentication' is not.

faq.question: How many sprints should a plan have?
faq.answer: The number of sprints depends on complexity. Each sprint should result in a demoable, testable increment. Simple features may need 1-2 sprints, while complex systems might require 4 or more.

faq.question: What if I do not know all the requirements upfront?
faq.answer: The skill includes a clarification phase where it asks targeted questions about scope, constraints, priorities, and edge cases. Answer these honestly to improve plan quality even with incomplete initial requirements.

faq.question: Can I modify the plan after it is generated?
faq.answer: Yes. The plan is a starting point. Update it as you learn more during implementation, encounter unexpected issues, or discover better approaches.
